record.field: Wiki.js is a modern, powerful open-source wiki engine for building documentation sites and team knowledge bases.

Wiki.js is a modern, powerful open-source wiki engine for building documentation sites and team knowledge bases.

Built on Node.js and Vue, it lets teams write content in Markdown, WYSIWYG, or raw HTML and organize it into a fully navigable, searchable wiki. It supports multiple databases and storage backends, fine-grained access control, and can mirror every change to a Git repository for portable, version-controlled documentation. Its extensive authentication and integration options make it a strong fit for organizations with existing identity and tooling.

Key features
  • Multiple content editors: Markdown, visual WYSIWYG, and raw HTML
  • Built-in full-text search with optional external engines (Elasticsearch, Algolia, and more)
  • Git synchronization to automatically version and back up all content
  • Broad authentication: local accounts, social login, LDAP, SAML, OAuth/OIDC, and 2FA
  • Granular page rules and group-based permissions for access control
  • Flexible deployment with PostgreSQL, MySQL/MariaDB, SQL Server, or SQLite and pluggable storage
Best for

Engineering teams, organizations, and communities that need a self-hosted, multilingual documentation portal or internal knowledge base with strong access control and Git-backed versioning.

field.name: tag_ids
field.eval: [Command.set([ref('agz_infra_suite.tag_self_hosted'), ref('agz_infra_suite.tag_documentation'), ref('agz_infra_suite.tag_cms'), ref('agz_infra_suite.tag_collaboration'), ref('agz_infra_suite.tag_productivity'), ref('agz_infra_suite.tag_search'), ref('agz_infra_suite.tag_git')])]

record.field: services: wiki: image: ghcr.io/requarks/wiki:2.5 restart: unless-stopped environment: - DB_TYPE - DB_HOST - DB_PORT - DB_USER - DB_PASS - DB_NAME depends_on: - db labels: - traefik.enable=true - traefik.constraint-label-stack=wikijs container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}' ports: - '{{ PORT_HTTP }}:3000' networks: - internal - agz-proxy-network db: image: postgres:14 restart: unless-stopped environment: - POSTGRES_USER - POSTGRES_PASSWORD - POSTGRES_DB volumes: - ./wiki_db_data:/var/lib/postgresql/data container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_db' networks: - internal networks: internal: driver: bridge agz-proxy-network: external: true

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Dump consistente de PostgreSQL (servicio en marcha) → $BACKUP_DATA_DIR docker compose exec -T db sh -c 'pg_dump -U "$POSTGRES_USER" -d "$POSTGRES_DB" --no-owner -Fc' \ > "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.dump" echo "pg_dump OK ($(wc -c < "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.dump") bytes)"

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Levanta solo la DB, espera readiness y restaura docker compose up -d db for i in $(seq 1 30); do docker compose exec -T db sh -c 'pg_isready -U "$POSTGRES_USER"' && break sleep 2 done docker compose exec -T db sh -c 'pg_restore -U "$POSTGRES_USER" -d "$POSTGRES_DB" --clean --if-exists --no-owner' \ < "$RESTORE_DATA_DIR/db.dump" # Verificación: la DB no quedó vacía (sh -c con dobles comillas; $VAR del contenedor con \$) N=$(docker compose exec -T db sh -c "psql -U \$POSTGRES_USER -d \$POSTGRES_DB -tAc \"SELECT count(*) FROM information_schema.tables WHERE table_schema='public'\"") echo "tablas restauradas: $N" test "$N" -gt 0
